_test_notes.purpose: Non-standard tecnoLC2 wiring: sensor slots (ORP/temperature) match the standard layout, but the CONTROL slots (chlorination level) do NOT — they sit on the legacy c4 (write) / c164 (read) pair instead of the standard c10. This is a deliberate stress test for the 'propose _standard_tecnolc2() when >=2 anchors match' heuristic: with only orp+temperature anchors it would (wrongly) look standard; adding a chlorination_level anchor exposes the mismatch.
_test_notes.provenance: All numeric values are copied verbatim from the cc24018506_chlorinator comment in chlorinators.py (AstralPool/Fluidra Energy Connect, firmware 40, Issue #129, @luistf76): c170=597 (calibrated ORP, matches app), c177=676 (uncalibrated raw, ~79 mV high), c172=315 (31.5 degC), c16=740 (pH setpoint 7.40), c20=710 (ORP setpoint 710 mV). c164=60 (chlorination level read, legacy slot) is a synthetic addition — the real comment doesn't give a chlorination-level figure. No c165 (pH) and no live c185 (salinity) per the same comment ('no live pH-measured component', 'no live salinity').
_test_notes.expected_anchors_cli_misleading: --anchor orp=597 --anchor temperature=31.5 (2 anchors, BOTH match the standard 170/172 slots -> tool proposes a _standard_tecnolc2() call, which is WRONG for this device: the real profile needs the c4/c164 chlorination pair, c8/c16 + c11/c20 setpoint read/write split, and c245 boost — see chlorinators.py.)
_test_notes.expected_anchors_cli_correct: --anchor orp=597 --anchor temperature=31.5 --anchor chlorination_level=60 (adding the chlorination_level anchor: its best candidate is component 164, which contradicts the standard slot 10 (absent from this device) -> tool correctly falls back to an explicit DeviceConfig block instead of the terse call.)
